incorrects.description: Student did not select both A and C on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Equal groups means the same number in every group. Look at each picture again.

incorrects.description: Student did not select both A and C on second attempt

params.description: Image A highlights — 3 shelves, 6 jars on each shelf.
Image C highlights — 2 stacks, 9 books in each stack.

steps.text: Count carefully. The first picture has 6 jars on EACH shelf. The third picture has 9 books in EACH stack. Same count in every group — that's equal groups. Are there any others?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates counting Image A: shelf 1 shows 6 jars, shelf 2 shows 6 jars, shelf 3 shows 6 jars.

steps.text: Image A: I count the first shelf — 6 jars. Second shelf — 6 jars. Third shelf — 6 jars. Every shelf has the same number. That's equal groups.

params.description: System demonstrates counting Image C: stack 1 shows 9 books, stack 2 shows 9 books.

steps.text: Image C: First stack — 9 books. Second stack — 9 books. Same number in both. Equal groups. A and C show equal groups. We can write multiplication equations for pictures with equal groups.
